# Pattern Matching Test - Verify Output Pattern Validation
# Demonstrates: literal matching, regex patterns, negative patterns, complex assertions

# Test JSON output with literal matching
exec echo '{"status":"ok","code":200,"message":"Success"}'
stdout "status"
stdout "ok"
stdout "200"
stdout "Success"

# Test that error isn't present
! stdout "error"
! stdout "failed"

# Test compound patterns
exec echo '{"data":{"nested":{"value":"important"}}}'
stdout "nested"
stdout "value"
stdout "important"

# Test with special characters
exec echo 'URL: https://example.com/path?query=value&other=123'
stdout "https://"
stdout "example.com"
stdout "query=value"

# Test multiline output matching
exec sh -c 'echo "Line 1"; echo "Line 2"; echo "Line 3"'
stdout "Line 1"
stdout "Line 2"
stdout "Line 3"

# Test line boundaries
exec echo "This is a test line"
stdout "test line"
! stdout "test line more"

# Test with numbers and operators
exec echo "Result: 42 > 0 && status != error"
stdout "42"
stdout "status"
